mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint high readings yield positive returns in more than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its twelve-month moving average confirms supply-side momentum, triggering short-term algorithmic buying and positive five-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during post-shock recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding two percent annualized sustains corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of quarters following GDP prints above two percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above three point five percent trigger immediate equity sell-offs as markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate in more than 55 percent of observations when CPI exceeds three point five percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor demand, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining for three consecutive months signals a leading downturn in construction and durable goods demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines coincide with positive forward returns in more than 50 percent of cases during late-cycle phases.
